q: What is the primary purpose of a text editor in assembly programming?
opts: To allow entry and preparation of programs into a computer-readable form
a: To allow entry and preparation of programs into a computer-readable form

q: Which file extension is the standard convention for programs written in assembly language?
According to the module, which assembler searches for the .ASM extension by default?
What is the function of an Assembler?
opts: Placing instructions in memory for execution
Saving ASCII text files to a disk
Translating assembly language mnemonics into machine code
a: Translating assembly language mnemonics into machine code

q: Why is the portability of assembly language mnemonics generally limited?
opts: Because text editors produce pure ASCII text
Because they are limited to one particular microprocessor
Because object code is too complex
a: Because they are limited to one particular microprocessor

q: Which program is responsible for placing machine instructions into memory at a specific starting address?
Which directive indicates how an operand or section of a program is to be processed?
Which memory model uses 64 KB for both the source code and the data code?
Which memory model uses 64 KB each (separate segments) for source code and data code?
What does the org 100h directive represent?
opts: The origin, indicating the start of a program
a: The origin, indicating the start of a program

q: Which segment is used to hold the actual program instructions?
To display a single character using INT 21h, what value must be placed in the AH register?
When using Service 02h of INT 21h, which register must hold the character to be output?
What is the service number used to display a string of characters using INT 21h?
To display a string, the offset address must be stored in which register?
Service 09h will display characters until it encounters which terminator?
What is the service number and interrupt used to clear the screen?
Which INT 21h function accepts a character from the keyboard and echoes it on the screen?
Which function is used for password protection because it does NOT echo the character and ignores Ctrl-Break?
Function 08h of INT 21h works similarly to 01h, except for what difference?
opts: It does not echo the character on the screen
a: It does not echo the character on the screen

q: What is the decimal value used in register DL for a Carriage Return?
What is the decimal value used in register DL to trigger a New Line?
In the syntax var_name db "string$", what does db stand for?
Which Interrupt is used to terminate a program properly without a specific service number?
If using INT 21h to terminate a program, what service number must be in AH?
Besides TASM, which specific program is mentioned for linking an assembly program?
Which editor is explicitly suggested in the module objectives for creating assembly programs?
According to the text, what is the prerequisite for the assembler and loader to process inputs?
opts: The inputs must be entered in real-time
The inputs must be saved files from the disk
The inputs must be in binary format
The inputs must be printed on paper
a: The inputs must be saved files from the disk

q: What is a text editor capable of producing, as required by assembly programs?
Today’s assemblers do much more than just translate mnemonics into what?
Where must machine instructions be placed before the microprocessor can execute them?
opts: In memory accessible to the microprocessor
a: In memory accessible to the microprocessor

q: The loader takes which specific type of code to place in memory?
What can be said about the range of complexity for loaders?
opts: They range from very simple to very complex
They are standardized and never vary
a: They range from very simple to very complex

q: Which memory model uses exactly 64 KB total for both source and data?
Which directive indicates the end of a program or a specific section?
Why can strings usually NOT fit in a single register?
opts: Strings are stored in the assembler
Strings are generally too large for registers
a: Strings are generally too large for registers

q: When passing a string address to registers, which register holds the segment address?
Which register holds the offset address of the first character of a string?
What is the hexadecimal ASCII value of the string terminator "$"?
In the syntax var_name db "string$", what does the dollar sign ($) represent?
Which INT 21h function is specifically noted for being useful for entering passwords?
What is a key difference between Function 01h and Function 07h?
opts: 07h echoes to the screen; 01h does not
01h echoes to the screen; 07h does not
a: 01h echoes to the screen; 07h does not

q: Function 08h is described as working like Function 01h, except for which detail?
opts: It does not respond to mouse clicks
The entered character does not echo on the screen
a: The entered character does not echo on the screen

q: To clear the screen using INT 10h, what value is stored in the AX register?
Which register is used to hold the service numbers for Carriage Return (13) and New Line (10)?
Which instruction is mentioned as something students should apply in Module 6.3?
One objective is to create a program that provides a simple screen output using which service?
Service 02h of INT 21H is specifically used for what?
If a character is not present in the keyboard buffer, what does Function 01h do?
What is the main purpose of "Carriage Return" ($DL=13$) in formatting?
opts: To move the cursor to the start of the current line
To move the cursor to the next line
a: To move the cursor to the start of the current line
